Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jul-27-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: Plaintiff FREDERICK SHIPMAN's motion for leave to file first amended complaint.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's unopposed motion for leave to file first amended complaint is granted.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Protective Order
Off Calendar Jun-28-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. PHIL THOMAS, Esq. of Knight Law Group, LLP (Tel: 310-552-2250) for plaintiff;
Atty. SARAH LAMBERT, Esq. of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP (Tel: 415-986-5900) for defendant Ford Motor Company.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY's Motion For Entry Of Protective Order.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

THE MOTION OF DEFENDANTS, FUTURE FORD OF CONCORD LLC, AND FORD MOTOR COMPANY (HEREAFTER EITHER "DEFENDANTS" OF "FORD") FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS:
(1) THE COURT WILL ISSUE THE PROTECTIVE ORDER SUBMITTED BY FORD WITH THE ADDED PROVISION ALLOWING THE ACCESS TO AND/OR DISCLOSURE OF CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL DESIGNATED AS "COMFIDENTIAL" TO MOCK JURORS AS PROVIDED IN PARAGRAPH 7(g) OF THE OF THE LASC MODEL PROTECTIVE ORDER.
(2) COUNSEL FOR FORD SHOULD PREPARE A PROPOSED PROTECTIVE ORDER, SUBMIT A COPY TO COUNSEL TO DETERMINE IF HE WILL STIPULATE TO ITS ENTRY AND DELIVER IN WORD FORM A COPY TO THE COURT (rennepar85@gmail.com)

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production From Defendant Ford Motor Company, And Request For Sanctions; Memorandum In Support Of Motion
Off Calendar Jun-28-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. PHIL THOMAS, Esq. of Knight Law Group, LLP (Tel: 310-552-2250) for plaintiff;
Atty. SARAH LAMBERT, Esq. of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP (Tel: 415-986-5900) for defendant Ford Motor Company.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF FREDERICK SHIPMAN's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production From Defendant Ford Motor Company, And Request For Sanctions.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court rules as follows: Plaintiff's motion to compel is denied.

Counsel for the prevailing party to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at rennepar85@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
